CONTENT: 1️⃣ Understand the Change
Read pull request description
Confirm the change solves the intended problem
Check linked issue or ticket for context
Confirm scope of changes is appropriate
2️⃣ Code Quality
Check for TODO or FIXME comments left in code
Check for hard-coded values or magic numbers
Confirm meaningful variable and method names
Confirm code readability and structure
Check for duplicate or unnecessary code
3️⃣ Style & Standards
Ensure linting or static analysis rules pass
Confirm code formatting is consistent
Verify naming conventions are followed
Check comments are clear and helpful
4️⃣ Architecture & Design
Confirm design aligns with existing architecture
Check separation of concerns is maintained
Verify dependencies are appropriate
Check for unnecessary complexity
5️⃣ Testing
Confirm appropriate tests are included
Review test coverage of new behaviour
Ensure tests are meaningful and maintainable
Confirm tests fail if behaviour breaks
6️⃣ Reliability & Observability
Confirm service includes health checks where appropriate
Verify logging is meaningful and not excessive
Check error handling and failure paths
Ensure retry or resilience patterns are used appropriately
7️⃣ Configuration & Security
Check secrets are not committed to code
Ensure configuration values are externalised
Verify environment-specific configuration is handled correctly
Check dependencies for security concerns
8️⃣ Performance Considerations
Check for inefficient loops or queries
Ensure large objects or data are handled efficiently
Confirm async patterns are used correctly where appropriate
9️⃣ Deployment & Operations
Confirm infrastructure changes are included if required
Check monitoring and alerts are considered
Ensure backward compatibility if required
Confirm migration or rollout considerations
🔟 Final Review
Confirm code is understandable by other engineers
Leave constructive feedback in PR comments
Approve PR or request changes
